title: Trump weighs ground forces, uranium-seizure raid as Friday Iran-war deadline looms
contents:
# Trump weighs ground forces, uranium-seizure raid as Friday Iran-war deadline looms

## Overview
The Independent live blog reported during the evening of April 30, 2026 that President Trump received an updated military-options briefing covering ground forces, additional precision strikes, and a special-forces raid to seize Iranian highly-enriched uranium, ahead of a Friday deadline either to end the war or formally extend it via Congress. The reporting circulated within the 21:00-22:11 UTC window.

## Reported Options Under Review
- Continuation or expansion of the existing US naval blockade of Iran
- Renewed precision strikes against IRGC and nuclear-program nodes
- Insertion of US ground forces into the operational area
- Special-forces raid to seize stocks of highly-enriched uranium
- Pause-and-negotiate posture pending Iranian concessions

## Political Context
The Friday deadline reflects War Powers Resolution timing pressure on the Trump administration to either secure a halt or transmit a formal continuation request to Congress. The decision arrives as oil prices reached $126 per barrel during the evening session and as Senator Susan Collins (R-Maine) joined Democrats earlier in the day on a measure to constrain unilateral war powers.

## Strategic Implications
A ground-force or uranium-seizure option dramatically raises escalation risk against the IRGC, while continuation of the blockade preserves a slower-burn pressure track. Analysts watching the National Security Council process flag that any briefed option crossing the Friday threshold would mark the first major US-led ground operation against Iran in the modern era.

## Tags
us, iran, trump, war-powers, ground-forces, uranium, blockade, congress
summary: President Trump received an updated Iran-war options briefing on April 30, 2026 covering blockade continuation, additional strikes, ground-force insertion, and a uranium-seizure raid, ahead of a Friday deadline to halt or extend the war via Congress.
